steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the scale factor between the similar parallelograms
steps.content: Since each side of parallelogram PQRS is 2 times the length of the corresponding side of ABCD, the scale factor is $k = 2$.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule for similar figures
steps.content:
When two similar figures have a linear scale factor of $k$, their areas scale by $k^2$. Therefore:
$$\text{Area of PQRS} = k^2 \times \text{Area of ABCD} = 2^2 \times 30 = 4 \times 30 = 120 \text{ square cm}$$
That is the correct answer.
steps.formula: $$\text{Area scales by } k^2 \text{ for scale factor } k$$
